name: Cinnamon-Raisin Swirl Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, heat milk until bubbles form around the edge of the pan; remove from heat.
2. Add 1/4 cup sugar, then salt and 1/2 cup butter; stir until butter melts.
3. Add mashed potato; cool to lukewarm. If possible, check temperature with thermometer.
4. Sprinkle yeast over water in a large mixer bowl; stir to dissolve.
5. Add milk mixture and 3 1/2 cups of flour; beat with an electric mixer until smooth (2 minutes).
6. Stir in raisins.
7. Gradually add remaining flour; mix in last by hand until dough is stiff and leaves side of bowl.
8. Turn dough onto a lightly floured pastry cloth or board. Knead until smooth and elastic (10 minutes).
9. Place dough in a greased bowl; turn to bring up the greased side. Cover with towel; let rise in a warm place (85F), free from drafts, until doubled (1 1/2 hours).
10. Mix 1/2 cup sugar and cinnamon.
11. Turn dough onto lightly floured pastry cloth. Roll one half into a 16x8 inch rectangle.
12. Sprinkle with half of the cinnamon-sugar mixture.
13. From the narrow side, roll up in jelly roll fashion.
14. Pinch edges and ends together; tuck ends under.
15. Place in greased 9x5x3 inch loaf pan, seam side down.
16. Brush with 1 tablespoon butter. Cover with towel.
17. Repeat with other half of dough.
18. Let rise in a warm place, free from drafts, until sides come to top of pans and tops are rounded (1 hour).
19. Place oven rack in the middle of the oven. Preheat oven to 375F.
20. Brush each loaf with the rest of the butter.
21. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes or until tops are well browned. Remove from pan immediately; cool slightly on a rack away from drafts.
22. Sprinkle with granulated sugar. Serve warm. Makes 2 loaves.
